mnemonics.title: Rank-Nullity Formula
mnemonics.content: KRaNK + NULlity = DOMAIN: Remember 'KRaNK' has same letters as RANK, 'NULlity' has L for Null. K(ernel) + R(ange) = D(omain). Or: Kernel + Range = Domain Size.

mnemonics.title: Singular vs Non-Singular
mnemonics.content: SINGULAR = Squeezed/Collapsed (loses dimension), NON-SINGULAR = NORMAL (preserves dimension). S in Singular stands for 'Smaller output dimension'.

mnemonics.title: One-to-one Test
mnemonics.content: KERNEL ZERO: For one-to-one, Kernel must contain only zero vector. Think 'KISS' = Kernel Is Single (element).

acronyms.expansion: Kernel - Essential Representation of Null space
acronyms.usage: Used to test injectivity: Kernel has only zero → one-to-one
Quick test for injectivity in linear transformations

visualTips.visualization: Imagine flattening a 3D object onto a plane (2D) - information is lost. Like projecting 3D shadow on wall.
Imagine stretching/squashing/rigid rotation - shape changes but stays same dimension. Like stretching rubber sheet.
All input vectors that get crushed to zero output. In projection to x-axis, kernel is the y-axis (all points with x=0).
The 'image' or output space. For projection to x-axis, range is the x-axis (a line inside ℝ²).

analogies.analogy: Like taking a 3D movie and converting to 2D - you lose depth information. Cannot recover original.
Like translating or rotating an object - you can always reverse the operation to get back original.
The 'black hole' of the transformation - vectors that get annihilated/mapped to zero. The 'victims' of the transformation.
Think of rank as 'useful output dimension' - how many independent directions survive the transformation.
